headline: Netflix cofounder and chairman Reed Hastings to step down from board of directors
summary: Netflix cofounder and chairman Reed Hastings will step down from the streaming service’s board of directors in June when his term expires, the company said on Thursday.  Hastings plans to focus on philanthropy and other pursuits.  Hastings had served as Netflix’s CEO for more than 20 years until 2023 after taking over the role from his friend and fellow company co-founder Marc Randolph in the late 1990s.

Hastings plans to focus on philanthropy and other pursuits.  Hastings had served as Netflix’s CEO for more than 20 years until 2023 after taking over the role from his friend and fellow company co-founder Marc Randolph in the late 1990s.

headline: Netflix shares dive as revenue barely beats expectations
summary: Netflix shares plummeted more than 8 percent on Thursday as the TV streaming titan reported quarterly revenue of $12.25 billion, which slightly topped expectations.The share price plunge came despite Netflix reporting profit of $5.28 billion, which was boosted by a fee received for the termination of a deal to buy Warner Bros.

headline: Netflix stock drops on Q2 guidance, Reed Hastings' departure plans
summary: Streaming superpower Netflix (NFLX) may have crushed first quarter earnings ($1.23 per share vs. expected $0.66) and revenue estimates ($12.25 billion vs. expected $12.17 billion), but its stock is dropping in Thursday's after-hours trading after its second quarter forecasts fell short. Yahoo Finance Senior Reporter Brooke DiPalma comes on Market Domination Overtime to review the fresh earnings figures from the company, alongside news that co-founder Reed Hastings plans to leave the board in June.

headline: Netflix (NASDAQ:NFLX) Beats Q1 CY2026 Sales Expectations But Stock Drops
summary: Streaming video giant Netflix (NASDAQ: NFLX) announced better-than-expected revenue in Q1 CY2026, with sales up 16.2% year on year to $12.25 billion. On the other hand, next quarter’s revenue guidance of $12.57 billion was less impressive, coming in 0.6% below analysts’ estimates. Its GAAP profit of $1.23 per share was 8.5% below analysts’ consensus estimates.

raw.headline: Netflix (NASDAQ:NFLX) Beats Q1 CY2026 Sales Expectations But Stock Drops

On the other hand, next quarter’s revenue guidance of $12.57 billion was less impressive, coming in 0.6% below analysts’ estimates. Its GAAP profit of $1.23 per share was 8.5% below analysts’ consensus estimates.

headline: Netflix co-founder Hastings to exit as company mulls its next move; shares fall
summary: Netflix  Chairman Reed Hastings is leaving the streaming service he co-founded 29 years ago as the company regains ‌its footing after it lost its $72 billion deal for Warner Bros Discovery.  In ‌a letter to investors released on Thursday, Netflix said Hastings will not stand for re-election at ​its annual meeting in June and plans to focus on philanthropy and other pursuits.  "Netflix is growing ⁠revenues double-digits,  expanding margins in 2026 and gushing free cash flow," said LightShed Partners media analyst Richard Greenfield.

raw.headline: Netflix co-founder Hastings to exit as company mulls its next move; shares fall
raw.summary: Netflix  Chairman Reed Hastings is leaving the streaming service he co-founded 29 years ago as the company regains ‌its footing after it lost its $72 billion deal for Warner Bros Discovery.  In ‌a letter to investors released on Thursday, Netflix said Hastings will not stand for re-election at ​its annual meeting in June and plans to focus on philanthropy and other pursuits.  "Netflix is growing ⁠revenues double-digits,  expanding margins in 2026 and gushing free cash flow," said LightShed Partners media analyst Richard Greenfield.

headline: S&P 500 Settles At New Record High: Investor Sentiment Improves, Fear & Greed Index Moves To 'Greed' Zone
summary: The CNN Money Fear and Greed index showed improvement in market sentiment. Stocks rose, with S&P and Nasdaq hitting all-time highs. Trump predicts end to conflict and lower oil prices. Morgan Stanley and Bank of America report strong earnings. U.S. import and export prices rose, while the NY Empire State Manufacturing Index beat expectations. Most sectors on S&P closed negative, except for tech and consumer stocks. Dow down, S&P up, and Nasdaq jumps. Earnings results expected from PepsiCo, Netflix, and Abbott Labs. The Fear & Greed Index measures market sentiment based on fear and greed, with a current reading of 56.5 indicating greed. Index ranges from 0 to 100.
